title: Result - Poland
content:
Welcome to Poland, a vibrant country in the heart of Europe, stretching from the Vistula River to stunning mountains and coastal landscapes.
Home to 14 UNESCO World Heritage Sites and 16 National Parks, Poland offers activities like rafting in the south, hiking in bison-filled eastern forests, or kayaking through picturesque lakes and historic canals. Immerse yourself in culture with festivals, battle re-enactments and visits to medieval and modern castles. Indulge in unique Polish cuisine, from pierogis to smoked oscypek cheese.

## Unique food tradition
Bigos (hunter's stew): made from sauerkraut, fresh cabbage, various meats and mushrooms.
With the EU’s 6th largest economy, Poland thrives in industries like aviation, mining and shipbuilding, while leading in agricultural and manufacturing exports.

### Local legend or myth
The Black Madonna of Częstochowa: a revered icon of the Virgin Mary located in the Jasna Góra Monastery, with a legendary origin tied to miracles and divine intervention.

### Language of the Land
Polish is the only Slavic language to have preserved ancient nasal vowels, Ą and Ę.
castles are found in Poland, including the world’s largest, Malbork Castle.
St. Dominic’s Fair (Gdańsk)
